subject: General Awareness
question_text: Which of the following words appears first in the Preamble to the Constitution of India?

explanation.concept: The Preamble outlines the core values of the Constitution in a fixed sequence starting with 'Sovereign Socialist Secular Democratic Republic'.
step_by_step: Recall the sequence of key terms in the Preamble
Identify the first term in that sequence

why_correct.B: Correct as 'Sovereign' is the first term

subject: General Awareness
question_text: Which Article of the Indian Constitution is known as the 'Heart and Soul' of the Constitution?

explanation.concept: Dr. B.R. Ambedkar referred to Article 32 as the 'Heart and Soul' because it ensures enforcement of Fundamental Rights through judicial remedies.
step_by_step: Understand which Article provides remedies for Fundamental Rights
Identify the Article that empowers citizens to approach Supreme Court

why_incorrect.D: Deals with life and personal liberty

subject: General Awareness
question_text: If the President of India resigns, to whom is the resignation letter addressed?

explanation.concept: According to Article 56, the President submits resignation to the Vice-President.

subject: General Awareness
question_text: Who is the ex-officio Chairman of the Rajya Sabha?

explanation.concept: The Vice-President of India acts as the ex-officio Chairman of Rajya Sabha as per Article 64.

subject: General Awareness
question_text: Which Constitutional Amendment introduced reservation for women in Lok Sabha and State Assemblies?

explanation.concept: The 106th Constitutional Amendment Act (Nari Shakti Vandan Adhiniyam) provides 33% reservation for women.
step_by_step: Identify amendment linked with women reservation

subject: General Awareness
question_text: What is the minimum age required to become the Prime Minister of India?

explanation.concept: Prime Minister must be a member of Lok Sabha or Rajya Sabha; minimum age for Lok Sabha is 25.
step_by_step: Understand eligibility linked to Parliament membership
Recall minimum age for Lok Sabha

subject: General Awareness
question_text: Which house of Parliament has the exclusive power to introduce a Money Bill?

explanation.concept: Money Bills can only be introduced in Lok Sabha as per Article 110.

subject: General Awareness
question_text: Who appoints the Chief Justice of India (CJI)?

explanation.concept: The President appoints the CJI based on the collegium system recommendations.

subject: General Awareness
question_text: Which Constitutional Amendment Act gave constitutional status to Panchayati Raj Institutions?

explanation.concept: The 73rd Amendment Act (1992) established Panchayati Raj as a constitutional body.
step_by_step: Recall amendments related to local governance

subject: General Awareness
question_text: Which body replaced the Planning Commission of India?

explanation.concept: NITI Aayog was established in 2015 to replace the Planning Commission as a policy think tank.

subject: General Awareness
question_text: Which part of the Indian Constitution deals with Fundamental Rights?

explanation.concept: Fundamental Rights are enshrined in Part III of the Constitution, covering Articles 12 to 35.
step_by_step: Identify which part includes Fundamental Rights

subject: General Awareness
question_text: Which Article guarantees equality before the law and equal protection of the laws?

explanation.concept: Article 14 ensures equality before law and equal protection of laws, forming the basis of the Right to Equality.
step_by_step: Recall provisions under Right to Equality
Identify the Article providing general equality principle

subject: General Awareness
question_text: In which year was the Constitution of India adopted?

explanation.concept: The Constitution was adopted on 26 November 1949 and came into force on 26 January 1950.

subject: General Awareness
question_text: Who was the Chairman of the Drafting Committee of the Indian Constitution?

explanation.concept: Dr. B.R. Ambedkar chaired the Drafting Committee and played a central role in framing the Constitution.
step_by_step: Recall key figures in constitution drafting
Identify role of Drafting Committee chairman

subject: General Awareness
question_text: Which Article of the Constitution guarantees freedom of speech and expression?

explanation.concept: Freedom of speech and expression is guaranteed under Article 19(1)(a), a core fundamental right.

subject: General Awareness
question_text: What is the maximum strength of Lok Sabha as per the Constitution?

explanation.concept: Maximum strength includes elected members plus nominated Anglo-Indian members (now discontinued).
step_by_step: Recall constitutional provision for Lok Sabha strength

subject: General Awareness
question_text: Which amendment is associated with Municipalities (Urban Local Bodies)?

explanation.concept: The 74th Amendment Act provides constitutional status to urban local bodies.

subject: General Awareness
question_text: Which constitutional body is responsible for conducting elections in India?

explanation.concept: The Election Commission ensures free and fair elections under Article 324.

subject: General Awareness
question_text: Who appoints the Governor of a state in India?

explanation.concept: The Governor is appointed by the President under Article 155.

subject: General Awareness
question_text: Which of the following correctly describes the term 'Republic' in the Preamble?

explanation.concept: Republic implies that the head of state is elected, not hereditary.

subject: General Awareness
question_text: Which Fundamental Right is directly violated if a law discriminates between citizens solely on the basis of religion?

explanation.concept: Article 15 prohibits discrimination on grounds of religion, race, caste, sex, or place of birth under the Right to Equality.

subject: General Awareness
question_text: Under which Article can the Supreme Court issue writs for enforcement of Fundamental Rights?

explanation.concept: Article 32 empowers the Supreme Court to issue writs like Habeas Corpus, Mandamus, etc., for enforcement of rights.
step_by_step: Differentiate between Supreme Court and High Court powers

why_correct.B: Explicitly provides writ power to Supreme Court

subject: General Awareness
question_text: Which of the following is NOT a feature of the Indian Constitution's Preamble?

explanation.concept: Federalism is a structural feature of the Constitution but not explicitly mentioned in the Preamble.

subject: General Awareness
question_text: Which authority decides whether a bill is a Money Bill in India?

explanation.concept: The Speaker of Lok Sabha has the final authority to certify a bill as a Money Bill.

subject: General Awareness
question_text: Which body conducts elections to the offices of President and Vice-President of India?

explanation.concept: The Election Commission of India administers and conducts elections to constitutional offices.

subject: General Awareness
question_text: Which of the following statements about Rajya Sabha is correct?

options.text: It can be dissolved by the President
Its members are directly elected by citizens
It is a permanent body with staggered terms

solution.final_answer: It is a permanent body with staggered terms

explanation.concept: Rajya Sabha is a permanent body; one-third of members retire every two years.

subject: General Awareness
question_text: Which of the following Fundamental Rights is available only to citizens and not to foreigners?

explanation.concept: Article 19 rights are exclusively available to citizens, unlike some other rights available to all persons.
step_by_step: Differentiate rights for citizens vs all persons

subject: General Awareness
question_text: What is the tenure of the President of India?

explanation.concept: As per Article 56, the President holds office for a term of five years.

subject: General Awareness
question_text: Which schedule of the Constitution deals with the allocation of seats in the Rajya Sabha?

explanation.concept: The Fourth Schedule specifies allocation of seats in Rajya Sabha to states and union territories.
step_by_step: Identify which schedule covers Rajya Sabha seats

subject: General Awareness
question_text: Which institution acts as the guardian of the Constitution of India?

explanation.concept: The Supreme Court protects the Constitution through judicial review.
step_by_step: Identify institution with judicial review power

subject: General Awareness
question_text: Which authority administers the oath of office to the President of India?

explanation.concept: As per Article 60, the oath of office to the President is administered by the Chief Justice of India.

subject: General Awareness
question_text: Which of the following is a correct statement regarding the Vice-President of India?

options.text: He is elected by only Lok Sabha members
He is the head of the State
He acts as President in case of vacancy

solution.final_answer: He acts as President in case of vacancy

explanation.concept: The Vice-President acts as President when there is a vacancy due to resignation, death, or removal.

subject: General Awareness
question_text: Which of the following conditions must be satisfied for a person to be eligible for election as President of India?

options.text: Must be a member of Rajya Sabha
Must be at least 35 years old and eligible for Lok Sabha
Must be a judge of Supreme Court

solution.final_answer: Must be at least 35 years old and eligible for Lok Sabha

explanation.concept: Article 58 lays down qualifications: 35 years age and eligibility for Lok Sabha membership.

subject: General Awareness
question_text: Which body is responsible for advising the President in the exercise of his functions?

explanation.concept: Article 74 provides that the President acts on the aid and advice of the Council of Ministers headed by the Prime Minister.

subject: General Awareness
question_text: Which Article provides for the abolition of untouchability?

explanation.concept: Article 17 abolishes untouchability and forbids its practice in any form.

subject: General Awareness
question_text: Which of the following correctly describes the relationship between Fundamental Rights and Directive Principles?

options.text: Fundamental Rights override Directive Principles in all cases
They are complementary and aim at establishing welfare state
They are mutually exclusive and unrelated

solution.final_answer: They are complementary and aim at establishing welfare state

explanation.concept: Fundamental Rights and Directive Principles are complementary; together they ensure political and socio-economic democracy.

subject: General Awareness
question_text: Which schedule of the Constitution contains the Union, State, and Concurrent Lists?

explanation.concept: The Seventh Schedule divides powers between Union and States through three lists.

subject: General Awareness
question_text: Which authority has the power to declare a law unconstitutional in India?

explanation.concept: Through judicial review, the Supreme Court can declare laws unconstitutional.

subject: General Awareness
question_text: Which amendment added the words 'Socialist' and 'Secular' to the Preamble?

explanation.concept: The 42nd Amendment Act, 1976 added 'Socialist' and 'Secular' to the Preamble.

subject: General Awareness
question_text: Which Fundamental Duty was added by the 86th Constitutional Amendment?

options.text: To provide opportunities for education to children

solution.final_answer: To provide opportunities for education to children

explanation.concept: The 86th Amendment added duty of parents/guardians to provide education to children aged 6–14 years.

subject: General Awareness
question_text: Under which Article of the Indian Constitution is the Union Budget referred to as the 'Annual Financial Statement'?

explanation.concept: The Constitution defines the legal framework for the budget through Article 112.
step_by_step: Recall constitutional provisions related to finance.
Identify Article 112 as the key provision.
Understand it defines the Annual Financial Statement.

why_correct.B: Correct as Article 112 explicitly defines the budget.
